member.summary: Circularly write to the file. If write operation reaches the end of the file, start writing from the beginning of the file.
member.param: The buffer which contains the data to be written.
The count of bytes to be written.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.MemoryMappedFileHandler.TryGetLogStream(System.Int32,System.IO.Stream@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Try to get the log stream which is seeked to the position where the next line of log should be written.
member.param: The number of bytes that need to be written.
When this method returns, contains the Stream object where `byteCount` of bytes can be written.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.MemoryMappedFileHandler.GetStream
member.summary: Get a MemoryMappedViewStream for the MemoryMappedFile object for the current thread. If no MemoryMappedFile is created yet, return null.
member.returns: A MemoryMappedViewStream for the MemoryMappedFile object.
member.exception: Thrown when access to the memory-mapped file is unauthorized.
Thrown in a race condition when the memory-mapped file is closed after null check.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsConfigParser.ConfigBufferSize
member.summary: ConfigBufferSize is the maximum bytes of config file that will be read.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher
member.summary: SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher class checks a location for a configuration file and open a MemoryMappedFile of a configured size at the configured file path. The class provides a stream object with proper write position if the configuration file is present and valid. Otherwise, the stream object would be unavailable, nothing will be logged to any file.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener
member.summary: SelfDiagnosticsEventListener class enables the events from OpenTelemetry event sources and write the events to a local file in a circular way.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.EncodeInBuffer(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Encode a string into the designated position in a buffer of bytes, which will be written as log. If isParameter is true, wrap "{}" around the string. The buffer should not be filled to full, leaving at least one byte empty space to fill a '\n' later. If the buffer cannot hold all characters, truncate the string and replace extra content with "...". The buffer is not guaranteed to be filled until the last byte due to variable encoding length of UTF-8, in order to prioritize speed over space.
member.param: Whether the string is a parameter. If true, "{}" will be wrapped around the string.
The byte array to contain the resulting sequence of bytes.
The position at which to start writing the resulting sequence of bytes.
member.returns: The position of the buffer after the last byte of the resulting sequence.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.DateTimeGetBytes(System.DateTime,System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary:
Write the datetime formatted string into bytes byte-array starting at byteIndex position.
[DateTimeKind.Utc] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff Z (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412Z).
[DateTimeKind.Local] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff +|- HH : mm (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412-08:00).
[DateTimeKind.Unspecified] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412).
member.remarks: The bytes array must be large enough to write 27-33 charaters from the byteIndex starting position.
member.returns: The number of bytes written.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.OnEventWritten(System.Diagnostics.Tracing.EventWrittenEventArgs)
member.summary: This method records the events from event sources to a local file, which is provided as a stream object by SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher class. The file size is bound to a upper limit. Once the write position reaches the end, it will be reset to the beginning of the file.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsInitializer
member.summary: Self diagnostics class captures the EventSource events sent by Application Insights modules and writes them to local file for internal troubleshooting.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsInitializer.Instance
member.summary: Long-living object that holds relevant resources.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.Implementation.Tracing.SelfDiagnostics.SelfDiagnosticsInitializer.EnsureInitialized
member.summary: Trigger CLR to initialize static fields and static constructors of SelfDiagnosticsInitializer. No member of SelfDiagnosticsInitializer class is explicitly called when an EventSource class, say AspNetCoreEventSource, is invoked to send an event. This method needs to be called in order to capture any EventSource event.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.DisableTelemetry
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether sending of telemetry to Application Insights is disabled.
member.remarks: This disable tracking setting value is used by default by all instances created in the application.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.ConnectionString
member.summary: Gets or sets the connection string.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.SamplingRatio
member.summary: Gets or sets the sampling ratio for traces (0.0 to 1.0). A value of 1.0 means all telemetry is sent, 0.5 means 50% is sent. When null, rate limiting sampling is used instead.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.TracesPerSecond
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of traces per second for rate-limited sampling. Rate limited sampling is the default form of sampling.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.StorageDirectory
member.summary: Gets or sets the directory for offline telemetry storage. When null, the Azure Monitor Exporter default (system default location) is used.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.DisableOfflineStorage
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether offline storage is disabled. When null, the Azure Monitor Exporter default of false (offline storage enabled) is used.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.EnableLiveMetrics
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether Live Metrics is enabled. When null, the Azure Monitor Exporter default of true is used.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.EnableTraceBasedLogsSampler
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether trace-based log sampling is enabled. When null, the Azure Monitor Exporter default of true is used.

member.name: P:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.ExtensionVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the version string to report to SDK stats. Eg., "shc1.2.3".

member.summary: Gets the default ActivitySource used by TelemetryClient.

member.summary: Gets the MetricsManager used by TelemetryClient for metrics tracking.

member.summary: Allows extending the OpenTelemetry builder configuration.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.PrependOpenTelemetryBuilderConfiguration(System.Action{OpenTelemetry.IOpenTelemetryBuilder})
member.summary: Prepends a configuration action so it runs before any user-registered configuration. This ensures that enrichment processors (e.g., TelemetryContextLogProcessor) execute before export processors, regardless of the order users call ConfigureOpenTelemetryBuilder.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Extensibility.TelemetryConfiguration.SetCloudRole(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the cloud role name and role instance for telemetry. This configures the OpenTelemetry Resource with service.name, service.namespace, service.instance.id, and service.version attributes which map to Cloud.RoleName, Cloud.RoleInstance, and Application.Ver in Application Insights.
member.param: Optional. The service instance ID (maps to Cloud.RoleInstance). If not provided, defaults to hostname.
Optional. The service version (maps to Application.Ver).
member.exception: Thrown if the configuration has already been built.

member.summary: Builds the OpenTelemetry SDK with the current configuration. This is called internally by TelemetryClient and should not be called directly.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metric
member.summary:
Represents a zero- or multi-dimensional metric.
Contains convenience methods to track, aggregate and send values.
A Metric instance groups one or more MetricSeries that actually track and aggregate values along with naming and configuration attributes that identify the metric and define how it will be aggregated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metric.#ctor(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient,System.String,System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metric.TrackValue(System.Double)
member.summary:
Tracks the specified value.
An aggregate representing tracked values will be automatically sent to the cloud ingestion endpoint at the end of each aggregation period.
This method uses the zero-dimensional MetricSeries associated with this metric. Use TrackValue(..) to track values into MetricSeries associated with specific dimension-values in multi-dimensional metrics.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metric.TrackValue(System.Object)
member.summary:
Tracks the specified value.
An aggregate representing tracked values will be automatically sent to the cloud ingestion endpoint at the end of each aggregation period.
This method uses the zero-dimensional MetricSeries associated with this metric. Use TrackValue(..) to track values into MetricSeries associated with specific dimension-values in multi-dimensional metrics.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metric.TrackValue(System.Double,System.String)
member.summary:
Tracks the specified value using the MetricSeries associated with the specified dimension value.
An aggregate representing tracked values will be automatically sent to the cloud ingestion endpoint at the end of each aggregation period. This overload may only be used with 1-dimensional metrics. Use other overloads to specify a matching number of dimension values for this metric.

An aggregate representing tracked values will be automatically sent to the cloud ingestion endpoint at the end of each aggregation period. This overload may only be used with 10-dimensional metrics. Use other overloads to specify a matching number of dimension values for this metric.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor
member.summary: An activity processor that applies client-level properties to all activities as tags, using skip-if-present semantics. This ensures context tags are applied universally — to Track* calls, Start/Stop operations, and any OpenTelemetry API activities emitted by customer code.
member.remarks: Performance optimization: after a warmup period ( calls), context properties are frozen into a compact snapshot array. This avoids repeatedly navigating the TelemetryContext object graph and skipping null/empty values on every call. This relies on the documented pattern that customers set TelemetryClient.Context properties once during initialization.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.WarmupCountThreshold
member.summary: Minimum number of OnEnd calls before the context snapshot can be frozen.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.WarmupTimeThresholdMs
member.summary: Minimum time (in milliseconds) after construction before the context snapshot can be frozen. This guards against high-throughput apps where 10 activities complete before async initialization (e.g., IHostedService, middleware) has had a chance to set TelemetryContext properties.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.frozenTags
member.summary: Frozen snapshot of non-null context tags, built after warmup. Once set, this array is immutable and read lock-free.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.warmupCounter
member.summary: Counter tracking the number of OnEnd calls during warmup.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.#ctor(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.DataContracts.TelemetryContext)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.OnEnd(System.Diagnostics.Activity)
member.summary: Called when an activity ends. Applies client-level context tags using skip-if-present semantics: if a tag is already set (by item-level context, Track* methods, or instrumentation), it is not overwritten. This matches the 2.x SDK's Tags.CopyTagValue precedence behavior.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.ApplySnapshot(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}[])
member.summary: Applies the frozen snapshot of context tags to the activity. Only contains entries where the value was non-null/non-empty at snapshot time.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.SlowPathOnEnd(System.Diagnostics.Activity)
member.summary: Full context evaluation path used during warmup. After calls, builds and freezes the snapshot for all subsequent calls.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.BuildSnapshot
member.summary: Builds an immutable snapshot array of all non-null/non-empty context tags. Called once after warmup completes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextActivityProcessor.HasTimeThresholdElapsed
member.summary: Returns true if at least milliseconds have elapsed since this processor was constructed. Uses for monotonic, allocation-free timing.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor
member.summary: A log processor that applies client-level properties to all log records as attributes, using skip-if-present semantics. This ensures context attributes are applied universally — to Track* calls and any calls from customer code.
member.remarks: Performance optimization: after a warmup period ( calls), context properties are frozen into a compact snapshot array. This eliminates per-call allocations of and intermediate buffers, and avoids repeatedly navigating the object graph. This relies on the documented pattern that customers set TelemetryClient.Context properties once during initialization.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.WarmupCountThreshold
member.summary: Minimum number of OnEnd calls before the context snapshot can be frozen.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.WarmupTimeThresholdMs
member.summary: Minimum time (in milliseconds) after construction before the context snapshot can be frozen. This guards against high-throughput apps where activities complete before async initialization has had a chance to set TelemetryContext properties.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.frozenAttributes
member.summary: Frozen snapshot of non-null context attributes, built after warmup. Once set, this array is immutable and read lock-free.

member.name: F:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.warmupCounter
member.summary: Counter tracking the number of OnEnd calls during warmup.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.#ctor(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.DataContracts.TelemetryContext)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.OnEnd(OpenTelemetry.Logs.LogRecord)
member.summary: Called when a log record ends. Applies client-level context attributes using skip-if-present semantics: if an attribute key is already present in the log record, it is not overwritten. This matches the 2.x SDK's Tags.CopyTagValue precedence behavior. The Azure Monitor Exporter's LogsHelper.ProcessLogRecordProperties will pick up these attributes and route them into LogContextInfo for context tag mapping.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.ApplySnapshot(OpenTelemetry.Logs.LogRecord,System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.Object}[])
member.summary: Fast path: merges the frozen snapshot attributes into the log record, skipping any keys already present. Zero-allocation when the log record has no pre-existing attributes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.ContainsKey(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.Object}},System.String)
member.summary: Checks whether the attribute collection contains the specified key. Uses linear scan — attribute lists are typically small (<20 items).

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.SlowPathOnEnd(OpenTelemetry.Logs.LogRecord)
member.summary: Full context evaluation path used during warmup. After both thresholds are met, builds and freezes the snapshot for all subsequent calls.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.BuildSnapshot
member.summary: Builds an immutable snapshot array of all non-null/non-empty context attributes. Called once after warmup completes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Processors.TelemetryContextLogProcessor.HasTimeThresholdElapsed
member.summary: Returns true if at least milliseconds have elapsed since this processor was constructed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.TrackMetric(System.String,System.Double,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary:
This method is not the preferred method for sending metrics. Metrics should always be pre-aggregated across a time period before being sent.
Use one of the GetMetric(..) overloads to get a metric object for accessing SDK pre-aggregation capabilities.
If you are implementing your own pre-aggregation logic, then you can use this method. If your application requires sending a separate telemetry item at every occasion without aggregation across time, you likely have a use case for event telemetry; see .
member.param: Named string values you can use to classify and filter metrics.

member.summary:
This method is not the preferred method for sending metrics. Metrics should always be pre-aggregated across a time period before being sent.
Use one of the GetMetric(..) overloads to get a metric object for accessing SDK pre-aggregation capabilities.
If you are implementing your own pre-aggregation logic, then you can use this method. If your application requires sending a separate telemetry item at every occasion without aggregation across time, you likely have a use case for event telemetry; see .

remarks.para: This method implements a shim layer that converts Application Insights DependencyTelemetry objects into OpenTelemetry Activities. The Azure Monitor Exporter then processes these Activities to emit telemetry.

item.description: Messaging (Queue Message, Azure Service Bus, Azure Event Hubs) → ActivityKind.Producer

remarks.para: This method sets Microsoft override attributes to preserve exact Application Insights semantics. The Azure Monitor Exporter uses these attributes to populate RemoteDependencyData fields:

These override attributes ensure backward compatibility and prevent data loss during the OpenTelemetry migration. For Activities created by OpenTelemetry instrumentation (without override attributes), the exporter will map semantic convention attributes (url.full, db.system, messaging.system, etc.) to Application Insights fields.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.Track(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Channel.ITelemetry)
member.summary: This method is an internal part of Application Insights infrastructure. Do not call.

member.summary: Send information about a request handled by the application.
member.param: The time when the page was requested.
The time taken by the application to handle the request.
True if the request was handled successfully by the application.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.TrackRequest(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.DataContracts.RequestTelemetry)
member.summary: Send information about a request handled by the application. Create a separate instance for each call to .

remarks.para: This method implements a shim layer that converts Application Insights RequestTelemetry objects into OpenTelemetry Activities. The Azure Monitor Exporter then processes these Activities to emit telemetry.

item.description: Messaging requests (sb://, amqp://, servicebus, eventhub URLs or Source) → ActivityKind.Consumer

remarks.para: This method sets Microsoft override attributes to preserve exact Application Insights semantics. The Azure Monitor Exporter uses these attributes to populate RequestData fields:

These override attributes ensure backward compatibility and prevent data loss during the OpenTelemetry migration. For Activities created by OpenTelemetry instrumentation (without override attributes), the exporter will map semantic convention attributes (url.full, http.response.status_code, messaging.system, etc.) to Application Insights fields.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.Flush
member.summary: Flushes the in-memory buffer and any metrics being pre-aggregated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.FlushAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously Flushes the in-memory buffer and any metrics being pre-aggregated.
member.param: Cancellation token to control the flush timeout and cancellation.
member.remarks: Learn more The caller controls the timeout by creating a CancellationTokenSource with the desired timeout. Example: using var cts = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30)); await client.FlushAsync(cts.Token);.
member.returns: Returns true when all telemetry providers successfully flushed their data. Returns false if any provider failed to flush or if the operation was cancelled.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.GetMetric(System.String)
member.summary:
Gets or creates a metric container that you can use to track, aggregate and send metric values.
Optionally specify a metric configuration to control how the tracked values are aggregated.
member.remarks:
The aggregated values will be sent to the TelemetryConfiguration associated with this client.
The aggregation scope of the fetchedMetric is TelemetryConfiguration; this means that all values tracked for a given metric ID and dimensions will be aggregated together across all clients that share the same TelemetryConfiguration.
member.param: The ID (name) of the metric. (The namespace specified in MetricIdentifier.DefaultMetricNamespace will be used. To specify another namespace, use an overload that takes a MetricIdentifier parameter instead.)
member.returns: A Metric with the specified ID and dimensions. If you call this method several times with the same metric ID and dimensions for a given aggregation scope, you will receive the same instance of Metric.
member.exception: If you previously created a metric with the same namespace, ID, dimensions and aggregation scope, but with a different configuration. When calling this method to get a previously created metric, you can simply avoid specifying any configuration (or specify null) to imply the configuration used earlier.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.GetMetric(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Gets or creates a metric container that you can use to track, aggregate and send metric values.
Optionally specify a metric configuration to control how the tracked values are aggregated.
member.remarks:
The aggregated values will be sent to the TelemetryConfiguration associated with this client.
The aggregation scope of the fetchedMetric is TelemetryConfiguration; this means that all values tracked for a given metric ID and dimensions will be aggregated together across all clients that share the same TelemetryConfiguration.
member.param: The ID (name) of the metric. (The namespace specified in MetricIdentifier.DefaultMetricNamespace will be used. To specify another namespace, use an overload that takes a MetricIdentifier parameter instead.)
The name of the first dimension.
member.exception: If you previously created a metric with the same namespace, ID, dimensions and aggregation scope, but with a different configuration. When calling this method to get a previously created metric, you can simply avoid specifying any configuration (or specify null) to imply the configuration used earlier.
member.returns: A instance that you can use to automatically aggregate and then sent metric data value.

The name of the third dimension.
The name of the fourth dimension.
member.exception: If you previously created a metric with the same namespace, ID, dimensions and aggregation scope, but with a different configuration. When calling this method to get a previously created metric, you can simply avoid specifying any configuration (or specify null) to imply the configuration used earlier.
member.returns: A instance that you can use to automatically aggregate and then sent metric data value.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.TelemetryClient.GetMetric(Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Metrics.MetricIdentifier)
member.summary:
Gets or creates a metric container that you can use to track, aggregate and send metric values.
Optionally specify a metric configuration to control how the tracked values are aggregated.
member.remarks:
The aggregated values will be sent to the TelemetryConfiguration associated with this client.
The aggregation scope of the fetchedMetric is TelemetryConfiguration; this means that all values tracked for a given metric ID and dimensions will be aggregated together across all clients that share the same TelemetryConfiguration.
member.param: A grouping containing the Namespace, the ID (name) and the dimension names of the metric.
member.exception: If you previously created a metric with the same namespace, ID, dimensions and aggregation scope, but with a different configuration. When calling this method to get a previously created metric, you can simply avoid specifying any configuration (or specify null) to imply the configuration used earlier.
member.returns: A instance that you can use to automatically aggregate and then sent metric data value.
